sourceMovie.title: The Sting
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Sting works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Sting clicked for you, The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Sting?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, The Old Man & the Gun, Taxi Driver—each shares DNA with The Sting while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Sting?
faq.answer: The Sting (1973) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Sting part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Sting Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
